description: An ANN index ships with library-default parameters that miss true neighbors on the actual data.
body: Failure mode: an HNSW or IVF index is deployed with default M/ef or nprobe that under-explore the graph or probe too few clusters, so the approximate search misses relevant neighbors even though they exist. Detection signals: ANN recall well below brute-force exact recall on the same query set, and latency comfortably under budget (indicating room to search harder). Do not accept the gap as inherent to approximation. Safer fallback: sweep ef_search/nprobe against a labeled query set, plot recall versus latency, pick a setting that meets the recall floor within the latency budget, and rebuild with tuned build-time parameters if needed.
